hypothesis: Implementing a time-modulated electrolysis protocol—comprising high-current pulses (~330 mA cm−2 for 0.5 s) interleaved with low-current recovery intervals (~100 mA cm−2 for 1.5 s) under the champion 1.5 M KHCO3 / 0.75 M K2CO3 composition and optimized flow/temperature—will increase reactor yield by ≥20% relative to the best static condition (44 mA cm−2) by raising the time-averaged JCO without reducing CO2 utilization below 70%.
